name: the-truths-we-hold
description: Kamala Harris' memoir of her journey from a biracial childhood in Oakland to the U.S. Senate — a story of justice, family, and the fight to make America live up to its ideals, told through the lens of her work as a prosecutor, attorney general, and senator.
useCase: Understanding Kamala Harris' personal story and political philosophy, the role of prosecutors in criminal justice reform, immigrant family experiences, and the fight for progressive causes in the Senate.
knownRisks: Written before Harris became Vice President — does not cover her time in the White House
Campaign memoir — presents a curated, positive view of her record
Some passages are campaign-oriented rather than deeply reflective
Criticisms of her prosecutorial record (mass incarceration era) are not substantively addressed
